prompt: Create a warm retro-future vaporwave anime advertisement-style illustration of My Robo Soyokun, a cute household fan robot sitting with a family in a nostalgic Japanese living room on a sunny summer afternoon. The canvas is vertical 4:5, detailed hand-drawn 1980s anime / Ghibli-inspired linework with soft watercolor shading, golden sunlight, slightly faded film colors, and cozy clutter. Center the scene on one small cream-colored robot with a large round head, two orange circular glowing eyes, a tiny smiling mouth, blush cheeks, thin articulated arms, a rounded pedestal body, and an open fan-like structure behind it, implying it sends a pleasant breeze. Surround it with exactly four family members seated on tatami around a low wooden table: one adult man at left in a white sleeveless undershirt, one young boy beside him in a blue-and-white striped shirt, one younger child to the right of the robot in a pale sleeveless outfit, and one adult woman at far right in a light floral summer dress. Cover each human face with a plain medium-brown square censor block, four blocks total, while leaving hair, bodies, clothing, and poses visible. In the foreground, show exactly eight distinct table items: one large half watermelon on a plate in the center, three glasses of iced tea, one small black bowl, one small patterned cup or bowl, one yellow bowl of food, and one small dish near the watermelon. The room should have tatami flooring, a low wooden table, red-orange plaid curtains on both sides, a wide sliding glass balcony door with exactly four tall window panels, a dangling pull cord in the center, and a bright city apartment skyline outside with pale blue sky and fluffy clouds. Add retro home electronics and decor: a black stereo or radio on the left shelf, an orange-red vintage CRT television on the right, stacked speakers below it, small toys, posters or charms with Japanese-style doodles on the walls, and summer clutter. Use sunlit amber, faded teal sky, cream robot, red-orange curtains, warm wood tones; make the atmosphere nostalgic, wholesome, and slightly surreal, as if advertising a friendly robot appliance for every household. No modern devices, no photorealism, no readable English text, no logos, no watermark.
